definition_en: A general rise in prices over time, reducing purchasing power
example: High inflation is making everyday goods more expensive.

definition_en: A period of economic decline with falling output and rising unemployment
example: The country entered a recession after the financial crisis.

definition_en: Money put into something with the aim of making a profit
example: She made a smart investment in real estate.

definition_en: The percentage charged on a loan or paid on savings
example: The central bank raised interest rates to control inflation.

definition_en: Gross Domestic Product — the total value of goods and services produced by a country
example: The country's GDP grew by 3% last year.

definition_en: Money owed to another person or institution
example: The government is trying to reduce the national debt.

definition_en: A plan that shows how money will be spent over a period
example: We need to stick to our budget this month.

definition_en: A place where shares in companies are bought and sold
example: The stock market fell sharply after the announcement.

definition_en: Money given by the government to support a business or industry
example: The government provides subsidies to farmers.

definition_en: Money paid to the government based on income or purchases
example: Income tax is deducted from your salary each month.

definition_en: The income generated by a business or government
example: The company reported record revenue last quarter.

definition_en: The amount by which spending exceeds income or revenue
example: The government ran a large budget deficit this year.

definition_en: A person who owns shares in a company
example: Shareholders voted against the merger.

definition_en: A loan secured against property, repaid over many years
example: They struggled to pay the mortgage during the recession.

definition_en: Government policy of cutting spending to reduce debt
example: Austerity measures led to cuts in public services.

definition_en: A person who starts a business and takes financial risks
example: Many entrepreneurs struggle in their first year of business.

definition_en: The value of one currency compared to another
example: The exchange rate between the pound and the dollar has changed.

definition_en: The money remaining after all costs have been paid
example: The company made a profit for the first time last year.

definition_en: The legal state of being unable to pay one's debts
example: The company filed for bankruptcy after losing key contracts.

definition_en: Money borrowed that must be paid back with interest
example: She took out a loan to pay for her studies.

definition_en: A document requesting payment for goods or services
example: Please send the invoice to our accounts department.

definition_en: A basic product that can be bought and sold, such as oil or wheat
example: Oil is one of the world's most traded commodities.
